mode_descriptions.offline: Docker Compose; all services local; no internet after initial 6GB model download
mode_descriptions.online: Google Cloud Run + BigQuery Vector Search; NVIDIA L4 GPU for LLM

flows.name: RAG Query (POST /ask)
flows.purpose: Main pipeline. Demonstrates the augmentation step: query → embed → search → LLM-with-context.
step_outline: vector → orch: top-k hits with scores
orch → bielik: POST /api/chat with system+user (or single user online)

flows.name: Direct Query (POST /ask_direct)
flows.purpose: Baseline. Same model, same temperature, NO context. Shows hallucination.

flows.name: CSV Ingest (POST /ingest)
flows.purpose: Bulk-load 38 hotel rules. Embedding + upsert per row.

flows.name: Live Ingest (POST /ingest_text)
flows.purpose: Workshop demo: add a single rule and show the next /ask uses it. No restart.

flows.name: Auto Seed (startup)
flows.purpose: One-shot container loads CSV via /ingest then exits. Offline only.

lessons: Vector DB is the ONLY node whose label changes between modes. Everything else just swaps tech and port strings.
The seed node is mode-exclusive (offline only). The seed FLOW has onlyMode='offline' so users switching modes don't see a broken state.
Both LLM and embedding share port 11434 in offline mode because both run inside one Ollama container. In online mode they're separate Cloud Run services with different URLs.
Bielik's prompt structure differs by mode: offline uses {system, user} roles, online uses a single user message. This is a non-obvious mode difference worth highlighting on step 6 of the ask flow.

Score scales differ too: Qdrant returns similarity (0-1, higher = better), BigQuery returns distance (0-2, lower = better). Conversion max(0, 1-distance) happens in orchestration so the UI sees one scale.
